## [00:00 - 03:00]

Okay, now we're continuing on with the seed launch and we're talking about, now we're going to talk about the, well there's two ways to do this, we just talked about the product build out and now we're going to talk about this, the show me the money method. And this one's more complicated, it's harder to pull off, but it really does build your list and it makes you money at the same time. I've literally seen people build serious businesses from scratch by following this model. And I'm talking great, like six figure businesses within the first year. So this is the one where it's possible for there to be shenanigans involved and you want to avoid that and be ethical about this. Because it starts off similar to the product build out and that you line up a series of experts who are willing to be interviewed on a tele-seminar or a webinar. And the way they do this is they hopefully will offer maybe like 60 minutes of content and then pitch their product at the end of the call, you know take 10 minutes, 15 minutes to pitch their product at the end of the call. Usually the pitch is made through an affiliate link so you get a cut of the sales. So as you're doing this you're making money from affiliate sales so it's beautiful because you have the expert on your call and they're actually providing the content and you're getting paid an affiliate commission for any products that are sold. So you definitely want to, when you're lining up the experts you want to let them know it's part of a series that you're doing. You know you do something like this, you probably want to do six calls or, I mean you could do something like four calls but probably six calls or eight calls and you know you let the experts know that they're doing it as a part of a series. Now one of the things you can do, and this is where the potential slowness comes into play, is you use social proof of other experts that other experts are going to be on this webinar or tell seminar series and having experts will attract other experts. Now I've seen this backfire is where people went and they invited a bunch of experts and they didn't get a yes from any of the experts but they basically, you know they had Guru, ABC and D and they sent invitations to Guru, A, B and C and they went and they put photos of Guru, A, B and C on the page like Guru, all those Gurus had already said yes and they did it as a social proof way to get everyone else to, you know, the other Gurus on board. Now they're always going to be happy mailing their lists but again you can use the social proof routine that the other Gurus, you know, agreed to mail and they're going to mail. ## [09:06 - 11:45]

lines work is you can mute the line or you can unmute it. If you mute it, then people are only going to be able to hear you and any other presenters, anyone who calls in through the presenter line, the presenter pin code, that's the way they work. So as a presenter, you can mute the line where they can only hear you or you can unmute the line and then everyone can hear everyone else. And when you unmute the line, it's absolute chaos. There's always going to be, you know, someone's going to be yelling at their kid, someone's going to be flushing the toilet, someone's going to be having a conversation with someone else and you're going to hear all that. So even if there's only 10 or 20 people on the line, it's going to become, it'll be very noisy and you get, you get to hear every cough and sneeze, everything going on the background. So you don't want that. Now there are services that will allow you to mute and unmute individual lines. So someone can, if they hit a special code like a star one or a star two, it will raise their hand and you're going to be able to see that them raising their hand. And then you can individually unmute just their line. So you're not getting a lot of extra noise in the background. So you definitely want that capability if you're going to have any kind of interactivity at all. In tele-seminars, you know, you can use instant tele-seminar and go to webinar, both have that capability to unmute lines individually.
